reasoning: Muqtada al-Sadr formally announced a boycott of the 2025 parliamentary elections on March 27, 2025, and explicitly reaffirmed this decision on April 18, 2025, stating he would "never participate alongside the corrupt." While he has historically reversed election boycotts (most notably in 2021), the tight timeline for candidate registration works against a formal return. Candidate registration with the Independent High Electoral Commission (IHEC) closes on May 23, 2025. This leaves a very narrow window for Sadr to reverse his firm April stance and formally field candidates before the July 1, 2025 cutoff. Furthermore, internal sources anonymously floating an 'indirect path' (e.g., supporting independents rather than a formal Sadrist slate) as a compromise suggests that even if he influences the election, the movement will not formally announce that it is fielding candidates. Therefore, a YES resolution is highly unlikely, leading to an estimate of 14%.
